baseline_commit.description: [CLOSE] Formalize LENS real E2E pipeline execution baseline
baseline_commit.note: All future implementation streams are bounded relative to this commit. No stream may claim runtime authority over any artifact state that predates 93098cb without an explicit rebaseline contract.

LANE_A.name: Active Productized Generic Runtime
LANE_A.namespace_note: PSIG labels in Lane A are naming debt — these are generic distribution signals (DPSIG in Lane D). PSIG (LANE_A) != PSIG (LANE_D).
immutable_constraints: THRESHOLD=2.0 — locked in 75.x; no DPSIG stream may modify
RUN_RELATIVE_OUTLIER — activation method locked; DPSIG introduces parallel methods only
signal_registry.json — PSIG-sovereign; DPSIG writes to dpsig_signal_set.json only
binding_envelope.json — read-only for all downstream consumers
LANE_A.implementation_eligibility: AUTHORIZED for additive streams only — new artifacts, new scripts, no mutation of protected artifacts
LANE_A.mutation_authority: Requires explicit Lane A contract citing this manifest. Protected artifacts may not be modified without atomic Lane A preservation proof.

LANE_B.name: Legacy Static Markdown Reference
allowed_usage: Read-only reference for static telemetry field definitions (ST-XXX series)
Design authority for selector model and PSIG expansion candidates
Historical signal specification reference for BlueEdge
forbidden_usage: Invoke as live signal computation path
Cite as canonical signal provenance in implementation contracts
Import Lane B formulas into Lane A without explicit promotion contract
LANE_B.implementation_eligibility: NOT ELIGIBLE for implementation streams. Reference only.
LANE_B.promotion_path: Explicit promotion contract citing LANE_GOVERNANCE_LOCK.md required

LANE_C.name: Relational Forensic Prototype
allowed_usage: Forensic analysis of relational semantic concepts
Design inspiration for future Lane D PSIG derivation (no direct import)
forbidden_usage: Import R-PSIG formulas into Lane A or Lane D without explicit promotion contract
Reference in implementation contracts as pattern source
LANE_C.promotion_path: Lane C → Lane B promotion required before Lane B → Lane A promotion. No direct C→A path.

LANE_D.name: Target Consolidated Architecture

blocking_points.BP-01: psig_computation.json authorization not issued (40x.04 contract pending)
blocking_points.BP-02: PSEE pipeline not activated for any canonical client (canonical_topology.cluster_count=0)
blocking_points.BP-03: Sidecar adapter consumer target needs correction (must point to 75.x boundary, not Lane B)
blocking_points.BP-04: DPSIG namespace migration not performed (PSIG-XXX in Lane A must be renamed)
blocking_points.BP-05: PSIG-001..008 derivation code does not exist

allowed_usage: Define target architecture and namespace structure
Authorize additive migration steps via explicit contracts
Introduce DPSIG derivation as additive parallel pipeline (separate artifact, no Lane A mutation)
forbidden_usage: Cite as evidence that Lane A is incorrect
Allow architecture to leak into Lane A via informal implementation
LANE_D.implementation_eligibility: AUTHORIZED for additive streams. DPSIG additive derivation approved. Semantic activation BLOCKED until BP-01/BP-02 resolved.
LANE_D.semantic_authority_status: BLOCKED — semantic governance exploration formally closed (SEMANTIC_GOVERNANCE_EXPLORATION_CLOSURE.md). Reopen conditions R-01..R-05 must be met.

DISCOVERY_MODE.description: Broad codebase inspection, repository traversal, gap analysis, forensic investigation
allowed_behavior: Read any repository artifact for investigative purposes
Traverse directory structures to identify gaps
Run git log, git diff for historical analysis
forbidden_behavior: Declare implementation findings as governance truth without a closure document
DISCOVERY_MODE.traversal_scope: UNRESTRICTED — but findings must be explicitly documented before informing implementation
DISCOVERY_MODE.note: DISCOVERY_MODE is not IMPLEMENTATION_MODE. Findings from discovery must be formalized before use.

GOVERNANCE_MODE.description: Writing governance documents, manifests, architecture definitions, closure documents
allowed_behavior: Read all authoritative inputs declared in stream contract
Produce governance manifests and schema definitions
forbidden_behavior: Perform repository traversal beyond declared authoritative inputs
GOVERNANCE_MODE.runtime_access_scope: READ from declared inputs; WRITE to declared outputs

IMPLEMENTATION_MODE.description: Bounded execution only — creating scripts and artifacts within explicit contract scope
allowed_behavior: Read from ALLOWED_READS surfaces defined in this manifest
Write to ALLOWED_WRITES targets defined in this manifest
Create new scripts in authorized paths (scripts/pios/dpsig/)
Execute created scripts against declared validation fixtures
forbidden_behavior: Read from any artifact not in ALLOWED_READS
Write to any artifact not in ALLOWED_WRITES
Traverse scripts/pios/75x/ or scripts/pios/41x/ beyond reading declared constants
Perform generalized repository exploration to discover pipeline structure
Infer runtime behavior from undeclared artifacts
Hardcode client names or framework-specific logic in generic scripts
IMPLEMENTATION_MODE.traversal_scope: STRICTLY BOUNDED — only declared contract surfaces
IMPLEMENTATION_MODE.runtime_access_scope: READ from ALLOWED_READS; WRITE to ALLOWED_WRITES
IMPLEMENTATION_MODE.critical_rule: IMPLEMENTATION_MODE is not DISCOVERY_MODE. The execution perimeter is frozen before implementation begins. Implementation streams do not discover; they execute.

VALIDATION_MODE.description: Running scripts, checking outputs, comparing artifacts for correctness
allowed_behavior: Execute scripts declared in the implementation stream
Read output artifacts and compare against expected values
Assert TAXONOMY-01 field identity across replay runs
forbidden_behavior: Modify any artifact based on validation findings without a repair stream
Expand scope of validated scripts beyond declared behavior
Use validation findings to reinterpret the runtime baseline
VALIDATION_MODE.runtime_access_scope: READ only (no writes except designated validation artifacts)

CERTIFICATION_MODE.description: Signing off on completed streams, producing CLOSURE.md, issuing return blocks
allowed_behavior: Read all stream artifacts produced in IMPLEMENTATION_MODE and VALIDATION_MODE
forbidden_behavior: Certify incomplete validation (any VAL-check FAIL = no certification)
CERTIFICATION_MODE.runtime_access_scope: READ stream artifacts; WRITE closure documentation

allowed_reads.scope_note: Applies to IMPLEMENTATION_MODE and VALIDATION_MODE. This is the COMPLETE authorized read surface for streams in the DPSIG implementation family.

tier_1_runtime_inputs.mutation_rule: READ_ONLY — binding_envelope.json may never be written

authorized_for: Implementation contract reference — read before any implementation task
Deterministic replay validation — this fixture only; not a runtime specialization
tier_3_validation_fixtures.note: FastAPI is the canonical replay fixture. No client-specific logic may be derived from this path.

explicitly_forbidden_reads: scripts/pios/75x/* (except reading declared public constants — THRESHOLD, activation method names)
scripts/pios/41x/* (no read required for DPSIG derivation)
vault/signal_registry.json (PSIG sovereign; DPSIG does not read it)
docs/pios/40.4/*.md beyond declared static telemetry field definitions
docs/pios/40.5/*.md (Lane B — reference only; not an implementation input)
Any artifact not explicitly listed in tier_1, tier_2, or tier_3 above

allowed_writes.scope_note: Applies to IMPLEMENTATION_MODE. This is the COMPLETE authorized write surface for streams in the DPSIG implementation family.

authorized_for: DPSIG Class 4 implementation — new file only; additive
implementation_outputs.constraint: No imports of 75.x, 41.x, or psee_handoff modules. Stdlib only. Client-agnostic.
Separate artifact path; never written to vault/signal_registry.json

authorized_for: This governance stream's machine-readable manifest — this file

explicitly_forbidden_writes: vault/signal_registry.json — PSIG sovereign; DPSIG writes only to dpsig_signal_set.json
scripts/pios/lens_report_generator.py — protected Lane A artifact (reads dpsig_signal_set.json; must not be modified without explicit Lane A contract)
Any artifact under docs/pios/40.4/ or docs/pios/40.5/ — Lane B reference only
Any artifact outside declared allowed_writes paths above

CRITICAL.description: Any write to protected Lane A scripts (75.x, 41.x, run_client_pipeline.py, lens_report_generator.py)
CRITICAL.consequence: Immediate STOP — stream execution invalid; Lane A integrity breach

CRITICAL.description: Any change to THRESHOLD=2.0 in any 75.x script, or any DPSIG threshold that copies PSIG threshold value by reference
Writing any DPSIG entry into vault/signal_registry.json
Any signal that derives its activation_state from a PSIG activation outcome
Any implementation that claims authority over 75.x activation behavior
CRITICAL.consequence: STOP — semantic governance branch closed; reopen conditions not met

CRITICAL.description: Importing R-PSIG formulas, relational_signal_set.json patterns, or uuid-client artifacts into any Lane A or Lane D implementation

HIGH.description: Performing broad scripts/pios traversal or directory scans to discover pipeline structure during an IMPLEMENTATION_MODE stream
HIGH.consequence: Stream non-compliant — implementation may not proceed until perimeter is re-frozen

HIGH.description: Any 'if fastapi', 'if blueedge', or client-name-conditional branching inside a generic implementation script
HIGH.consequence: Implementation reject — script must be rewritten to be topology-native

HIGH.description: Reading any artifact not listed in allowed_reads during IMPLEMENTATION_MODE
HIGH.consequence: Implementation non-compliant — read must be justified and added to manifest or abandoned

HIGH.description: Inspecting run_client_pipeline.py or other Lane A orchestrators to discover execution order during implementation
HIGH.consequence: Non-compliant — execution order is declared in this manifest; inspection not authorized in IMPLEMENTATION_MODE

MEDIUM.description: Stream contract that does not declare LANE_SCOPE header
MEDIUM.consequence: Contract INVALID per LANE_GOVERNANCE_LOCK.md Rule NC-05

MEDIUM.description: Float operation that produces different results on CPython across platforms (e.g., unsorted summation, hash-order-dependent iteration)
MEDIUM.consequence: Signal fails REQ-02 (reproducibility) — must be corrected before shipping

MEDIUM.description: Signal entry in dpsig_signal_set.json missing any required derivation_trace field
MEDIUM.consequence: Signal fails REQ-06 (auditability) — must be completed before shipping

MEDIUM.description: Writing artifacts to paths outside ALLOWED_WRITES
MEDIUM.consequence: Implementation non-compliant — must be moved to authorized path

immutable_runtime_constraints.note: These constraints are frozen at baseline 93098cb. No implementation stream may override them without an explicit Lane A contract that supersedes this manifest.

constraints.name: THRESHOLD_LOCK
constraints.rule: No DPSIG stream may modify this value. DPSIG thresholds are defined independently in derive_relational_signals.py constants.

constraints.name: ACTIVATION_METHOD_LOCK
constraints.rule: PSIG activation method is frozen. DPSIG may introduce CLUSTER_MASS_THRESHOLD, ASYMMETRY_THRESHOLD, etc. as new methods, but may not alter the PSIG activation method.

constraints.name: SIGNAL_REGISTRY_SOVEREIGNTY
constraints.rule: DPSIG writes exclusively to artifacts/dpsig/<client>/<run_id>/dpsig_signal_set.json. No DPSIG entry may appear in signal_registry.json.

constraints.name: LANE_A_PIPELINE_ORDER
constraints.rule: Execution order is frozen. DPSIG runs in parallel at 40.5 — it does not modify the execution order of Lane A scripts.

constraints.name: BINDING_ENVELOPE_IMMUTABILITY
constraints.rule: No downstream consumer may write to binding_envelope.json. It is the canonical topology source.

constraints.name: SEMANTIC_ACTIVATION_BLOCKED
constraints.rule: Semantic activation authority is BLOCKED. Reopen conditions R-01..R-05 defined in SEMANTIC_GOVERNANCE_EXPLORATION_CLOSURE.md must be met before any semantic enrichment stream is authorized.

constraints.name: NAMESPACE_DEBT_ACKNOWLEDGMENT
constraints.rule: Any contract using PSIG must declare which definition applies. Namespace migration requires explicit Lane A migration contract.

implementation_freeze_rules.note: These rules apply to ALL future implementation streams in the PSEE ↔ PiOS family.

rules.name: PERIMETER_MUST_BE_FROZEN_BEFORE_EXECUTION
rules.rule: Implementation streams are NOT discovery streams. The execution perimeter (read surfaces, write targets, lane scope) must be declared in the stream contract before any implementation task begins. No perimeter expansion is allowed mid-stream.

rules.name: BASELINE_COMMIT_MUST_BE_DECLARED
rules.rule: Every implementation stream must explicitly reference baseline_commit=93098cb (or an authorized successor commit) in its contract header.

rules.name: LANE_SCOPE_MANDATORY
rules.rule: Every contract must include LANE_SCOPE and LANE_IMPACT headers. A contract without LANE_SCOPE is INVALID per LANE_GOVERNANCE_LOCK.md Rule NC-05.

rules.name: READ_SURFACE_FROZEN
rules.rule: Reads are bounded to the ALLOWED_READS declared in this manifest. Any read of an artifact not in allowed_reads requires a manifest amendment before proceeding.

rules.name: WRITE_TARGET_FROZEN
rules.rule: Writes are bounded to the ALLOWED_WRITES declared in this manifest. Any write to an artifact not in allowed_writes is FORBIDDEN without a manifest amendment.

rules.name: SCRIPTS_ARE_TOPOLOGY_NATIVE
rules.rule: All implementation scripts in the DPSIG family must be topology-native and client-agnostic. No client-specific logic (if-fastapi, if-blueedge, framework-specific branching) may exist inside any generic script. Client identity is a path parameter only.

rules.name: REPLAY_MANDATORY
rules.rule: Every implementation stream that produces a runtime artifact (dpsig_signal_set.json or equivalent) must include a replay verification run. overall_verdict=IDENTICAL is required for PASS.

rules.name: ADDITIVE_ONLY
rules.rule: DPSIG streams are additive. They introduce new artifacts and new scripts. They do not remove, rename, or repurpose any existing Lane A artifact. Non-regression is mandatory.

rules.name: DENOMINATOR_GUARDS_MANDATORY
rules.rule: Every signal formula that involves division must specify a denominator_guard. A signal without an explicit guard specification fails REQ-04 (Deterministic Derivation Basis).

rules.name: FAIL_CLOSED_MANDATORY
rules.rule: Scripts must exit with non-zero status on REQUIRED_ARTIFACT_MISSING or SCHEMA_VIOLATION. No partial output may be written on error. Fail-closed is a hard contract requirement.

pre_execution_checklist: STEP 1: Verify baseline_commit=93098cb is the declared root
STEP 2: Verify LANE_SCOPE and LANE_IMPACT headers are present
STEP 3: Verify all read surfaces are in allowed_reads or have explicit manifest amendment
STEP 4: Verify all write targets are in allowed_writes or have explicit manifest amendment
STEP 5: Verify no forbidden_patterns are present in the stream contract
STEP 6: Verify Lane A protected artifacts are listed as immutable
STEP 7: Verify DPSIG script contains no client-specific logic (IFR-06)
STEP 8: Verify replay verification is included in the stream tasks (IFR-07)
future_stream_enforcement.handoff_note: After PI.PSEE-PIOS.PIPELINE-EXECUTION-MANIFEST.01 PASS, resume PI.PSEE-PIOS.DPSIG-RUNTIME-NORMALIZATION.IMPLEMENTATION.01 within the perimeter defined by this manifest.
